user.title: PowerPilz Smart Helpers
user.description: Pick the kind of helper you want to create.

menu_options.schedule: Smart Schedule - weekly blocks of on/off windows
menu_options.event_schedule: Smart Event Schedule - weekly point-in-time triggers
menu_options.timer: Smart Timer - one-shot on/off at a specific time
menu_options.curve: Smart Curve - heating curve with weekly setpoint shape

schedule.title: Smart Schedule (blocks)
schedule.description:
Classic weekly schedule. You define time windows (e.g. Mon 18:00-22:00). During each window the target is held on; outside the windows it is held off.

Three override modes: Off (force off), On (force on), Auto (follow plan). Optional 'resume Auto at boundary' lifts a manual override at the next plan transition.

The weekly plan itself is edited in the PowerPilz Schedule card on your dashboard - long-press the card to open the weekly editor.

data.name: Name
data.restore_auto_on_boundary: Auto-resume Auto mode at next boundary

data_description.target_entity: The device to hold on during active windows and off outside. Supported: switch, light, input_boolean, fan, climate.
data_description.mode_off_name: Display name for the Off mode (forces the target permanently off).
data_description.mode_off_icon: Icon shown while in Off mode.
data_description.mode_on_name: Display name for the On mode (forces the target permanently on, ignoring the plan).
data_description.mode_on_icon: Icon shown while in On mode.
data_description.mode_auto_name: Display name for the Auto mode (target follows the weekly plan).
data_description.mode_auto_icon: Icon shown while in Auto mode.
data_description.restore_auto_on_boundary: If enabled, a manual On/Off override is lifted automatically at the next plan boundary. Handy if you often override short-term but forget to switch back to Auto.

event_schedule.title: Smart Event Schedule (events)
event_schedule.description:
Weekly point-in-time triggers. You set moments (e.g. Mon 18:00) and at each moment the helper fires the chosen action on the target device.

Three action kinds:
• Toggle - calls homeassistant.toggle on the target.
• Pulse - turns target on, waits N seconds, turns it off again.
• Custom service - calls any HA service you choose.

Two modes: Off (ignore scheduled events; manual trigger still works), Auto (events fire on schedule).

The helper also creates a companion `button.*_trigger` entity. Press it from any dashboard tile to fire the action manually - the button's history records every fire (scheduled or manual) as a single timeline.

data_description.target_entity: The device that receives the action at each event time. Supported: switch, light, input_boolean, fan, climate.
data_description.event_action:
What happens at every event moment:

• Toggle: calls homeassistant.toggle on the target. Flips its current state (off → on or on → off).

• Pulse: turns the target on, waits the duration set below, turns it off again. Perfect for doorbells, garage door buttons, watering valves, stairwell lights - anything momentary.

• Custom service call: calls any Home Assistant service you choose (see below).
data_description.pulse_duration_seconds: Only used when action is Pulse. Seconds the target stays on (1-900, max 15 min). After each pulse a fixed 10s cool-down silently drops any further trigger (scheduled or manual) so pulses never overlap.
data_description.event_service:
Only used when action is Custom service call. Service in `domain.service` form.

Examples:
• `scene.turn_on`
• `notify.mobile_app_pixel`
• `media_player.play_media`
• `script.water_plants`

The Target device above is automatically passed as `entity_id` to the call.
data_description.event_service_data:
Optional extra payload as YAML/JSON object. Leave empty if the service needs no extra data.

Examples:

notify.mobile_app_pixel:
  message: 'Time to water the plants'
  title: 'PowerPilz'

media_player.play_media:
  media_content_id: 'http://...'
  media_content_type: 'music'

entity_id is auto-filled from the Target device - don't repeat it here.
data_description.mode_off_name: Display name for the Off mode. In Off the scheduled events are ignored; the companion button still fires manually.
data_description.mode_off_icon: Icon shown while in Off mode.
data_description.mode_auto_name: Display name for the Auto mode. Events fire at their scheduled times.
data_description.mode_auto_icon: Icon shown while in Auto mode.

timer.title: Smart Timer
timer.description:
Bundles a target device with autonomous on/off-time logic in a single switch entity.

💡 After creating this helper, set the on/off times via the PowerPilz Timer card on your dashboard. The Smart Timer drives the target device autonomously — no bridging automations needed.

The target can be a regular switch/light/input_boolean, OR a select/input_select (including a PowerPilz Smart Schedule). For select targets, you'll be asked next which option to apply at each boundary.

data_description.target_entity: The switch, light, input_boolean, select or input_select this Smart Timer controls.
data_description.timer_direction: 'Both' turns the device on at the start time and off at the end time. 'On only' turns on at the start time and deactivates (no off event). 'Off only' turns off at the end time (no on event).
data_description.state_inactive_name: Label shown by the Lovelace card when the timer isn't running.
data_description.state_inactive_icon: Icon shown while the timer is idle.
data_description.state_active_name: Label shown while the timer is running.
data_description.state_active_icon: Icon shown while the timer is running.

timer_options.title: Timer select-target options
timer_options.description:
The target `{target}` is a multi-state entity. Pick which option the timer sets at each boundary.

Available options: {options}

data_description.on_option: The option to apply at the start time.
data_description.off_option: The option to apply at the end time.

curve.title: Smart Curve
curve.description:
Drives one or more heating targets with a weekly value curve. Edit the curve in the PowerPilz Heating Curve card on your dashboard — long-press the card to open the editor.

In Auto mode the helper samples the curve at the configured cadence and writes the result to every target (climate → set_temperature, number → set_value). On mode forces a fixed boost value; Off turns climate targets off.

data_description.target_entities: Climate or number entities the curve writes to. The same value is applied to every target.
data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often the curve is sampled and the targets are updated. Default 15 minutes.
data_description.value_min: Lower bound of the editor Y-axis and the value clamp.
data_description.value_max: Upper bound of the editor Y-axis and the value clamp.
data_description.mode_on_value: Constant value written while the helper is in On mode.
data_description.same_for_all_days: When enabled the editor only shows one day; the curve is mirrored across all weekdays.

error.duplicate_mode_names: The three mode names must be different.
error.targets_required: Pick at least one heating target.
error.invalid_range: Maximum must be greater than minimum.
error.invalid_event_service_data: Custom service data must be a dict.

abort.already_configured: This configuration is already set up.

schedule.title: Smart Schedule (blocks) options
schedule.description: Adjust the helper settings. The weekly plan itself is edited in the PowerPilz Schedule card on your dashboard - long-press the card to open the weekly editor.

data.name: Name
data.restore_auto_on_boundary: Auto-resume Auto mode at next boundary

data_description.target_entity: The device that's held on during active windows and off outside.
data_description.restore_auto_on_boundary: A manual On/Off override is automatically lifted at the next plan boundary.

event_schedule.title: Smart Event Schedule (events) options
event_schedule.description: Adjust the helper settings. The weekly events are edited in the PowerPilz Event Schedule card on your dashboard - long-press the card to open the editor.

data_description.target_entity: The device that receives the action at each event moment.
data_description.pulse_duration_seconds: Pulse only. Seconds the target stays on (1-900). 10s fixed cool-down after each pulse.
data_description.event_service: Custom only. Service in `domain.service` form. entity_id is auto-passed from the target above.
data_description.event_service_data: Custom only. Optional extra service-data dict.

timer.title: Smart Timer options
timer.description: Adjust the helper settings. The on/off times are set via the PowerPilz Timer card on your dashboard. If the target is a select entity, you'll be asked next which option to apply at each boundary.

data_description.target_entity: The switch, light, input_boolean, select or input_select this Smart Timer controls.
data_description.timer_direction: 'Both' turns the device on at start and off at end. 'On only' turns on at start and then deactivates. 'Off only' turns off at end.
data_description.state_inactive_name: Label shown by the Lovelace card when the timer isn't running.
data_description.state_inactive_icon: Icon shown while the timer is idle.
data_description.state_active_name: Label shown while the timer is running.
data_description.state_active_icon: Icon shown while the timer is running.

timer_options.title: Timer select-target options
timer_options.description:
The target `{target}` is a multi-state entity. Pick which option the timer sets at each boundary.

Available options: {options}

data_description.on_option: The option to apply at the start time.
data_description.off_option: The option to apply at the end time.

curve.title: Smart Curve options
curve.description: Adjust the helper settings. The curve itself is edited in the PowerPilz Heating Curve card on your dashboard — long-press the card to open the editor.

set_timer.name: Set timer on/off times
set_timer.description: Update the on and/or off datetime of a PowerPilz Smart Timer.

on.name: On time
on.description: ISO 8601 datetime (e.g. 2026-04-19T18:30:00). Omit to leave unchanged.

off.name: Off time
off.description: ISO 8601 datetime. Omit to leave unchanged.

set_schedule_blocks.name: Set Smart Schedule weekly blocks
set_schedule_blocks.description: Replace the weekly schedule blocks of a Smart Schedule helper.

blocks.name: Weekly blocks
blocks.description: Per-weekday list of time blocks. Each entry has `from` and `to` times (HH:MM:SS) plus an optional `data` payload.

set_curve_points.name: Set Smart Curve weekly points
set_curve_points.description: Replace the weekly curve points of a Smart Curve helper.

points.name: Weekly points
points.description: Per-weekday list of curve points. Each entry has a `time` (HH:MM:SS) and a numeric `value`.

set_schedule_events.name: Set Smart Schedule weekly events
set_schedule_events.description: Replace the weekly point-in-time events of a Smart Schedule helper that is in events mode.

events.name: Weekly events
events.description: Per-weekday list of events. Each entry has a `time` (HH:MM:SS) and an optional `data` payload.

trigger_event_now.name: Trigger Smart Schedule event now
trigger_event_now.description: Manually fire the configured event action on a Smart Schedule helper in events mode. Works in Auto and Off modes; suppressed during pulse cool-down.
